reasoning:
The recent historical baseline for riots in Seychelles is roughly 1 per year (an average of 0.083 per 30-day period), reflecting a highly peaceful political environment. Even a major transition of power to the opposition in 2020 occurred without reported riots. To trigger a YES resolution, at least 1 riot must occur in the corresponding 30-day windows. 

For the September 7, 2025 resolution date, the 30-day window (August 8 - September 7) is mostly in the past. Extensive web searches of regional and local reporting indicate no riots occurred in August. With only 7 days left, the likelihood is extremely small (~0.06). 

The September 30, 2025 resolution date covers the final stretch of the presidential election campaigns and the voting period itself (September 25-27). Although Seychelles has a strong democratic track record, elections inherently raise the likelihood of isolated tensions or minor scuffles between supporters (which could be categorized by ACLED as a 'riot' if 3+ members cause damage/fights). The probability is elevated to reflect this risk (~0.19). 

For the distant dates—November 29, 2025, and February 27, 2026—there will be no significant election activities, but forecasting horizons dictate standard uncertainty dragging calculations toward 0.5. These are estimated at 0.25 and 0.31, reflecting the mandate to increase uncertainty with time horizon.
